title: Jonathan D. See
content:
Partner | Co-Head of M&A and Co-Head of Private Equity
People.ContactEmail jsee@mccarthy.ca
- Osgoode Hall Law School
Jonathan See is one of Canada’s leading M&A and private equity lawyers, advising many of the world’s largest sponsors and strategics on transformative deals in Canada and internationally.
Jonathan is a partner in Toronto and Co-Head of the firm’s national M&A and Private Equity Groups. One of Canada’s leading dealmakers, he advises on sophisticated domestic and cross-border mandates, including some of the most significant and complex matters in the Canadian market.
His experience spans a broad range of industries, including energy, infrastructure, technology, mining and real estate, with significant experience on digital infrastructure in establishing asset management platforms and other strategically important alliances, including Instar Asset Management, Slate Asset Management, Magris Performance Materials, Genesis Financial Asset Management, among others. Clients value his commercial judgment and practical approach on high-stakes and market-defining transactions.
Jonathan is widely recognized as a trusted advisor on complex M&A and private equity matters and is consistently ranked by Chambers & Partners and recognized by Legal 500, IFLR, Lexpert and Best Lawyers . He also led Canadian Law Awards ’ M&A Deal of the Year for Brookfield Infrastructure’s C$16 billion acquisition of Inter Pipeline and was named a Lexpert Rising Star , recognizing Canada’s leading lawyers under 40.
Jonathan’s select experience, listed from most recent, includes:

#### INFRASTRUCTURE, ENERGY,  MINING & REAL ESTATE
- Brookfield Asset Management in its definitive arrangement agreement to acquire Boralex Inc., in partnership with La Caisse
- The consortium led by Instar Asset Management and including Investissement Québec and the Fonds de solidarité FTQ, in its acquisition of Groupe Somavrac Inc.
- Macquarie Asset Management in its acquisition of a 55% stake in Bristol Airport and a 26.5% stake in Birmingham Airport from Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan
- Energy Capital Partners on its investment in Green Infrastructure Partners in connection with its C$4.25 billion recapitalization
- Brookfield Asset Management in its equity financing of 5C, as part of 5C’s US$835 million equity and debt financing
- Co‑investors in a group led by Blackstone on a C$7 billion equity investment in Rogers Communications Inc.
- CVC DIF in its strategic partnership with Mattamy Homes, through its portfolio company Diverso Energy
- Brookfield Asset Management in its acquisition of a 53.12% stake in Neoen for £3.25 billion
- TD Asset Management in its joint investment with Fair Market Value Capital Partners to acquire a 28% interest in PSA Italy from PSA International, a leading global port operator
- SCF Partners in its acquisition of Entegris Inc.’s Pipeline and Industrial Materials business, consisting of Flowchem, Val‑Tex, and Sealweld
- Skyservice Business Aviation in their purchase of interests in Fontainebleau Aviation’s fixed‑based operators in Miami Opa‑Locka and Fort Lauderdale‑Hollywood Airports
- Brookfield Asset Management and Evoque Data Center Solutions in its US$1.3 billion acquisition of Cyxtera Technologies
- Brookfield Asset Management and Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan in its acquisition of Compass Datacenters from RedBird Capital Partners and Azrieli Group
- CVC Capital Partners in its acquisition of a majority stake of Scan Global Logistics
- CVC DIF in its acquisition of Diverso Energy
- Brookfield Asset Management in its agreement to buy HomeServe PLC for £4.08 billion
- Blackstone and the Benettons, in their €58 billion buyout offer for Atlantia
- Enercare in the acquisition of all of the issued and outstanding units of HydroSolution L.P. and HydroSolution Ltd.
- Brookfield Asset Management investing through its super core‑infrastructure fund, together with Alecta, acquired 49% of Telia Company’s tower business in Sweden, expanding the partnership where Brookfield and Alecta acquired 49% of Telia’s tower business in Finland and Norway
- PTV Group in its acquisition of a majority stake in Econolite
- Brookfield Asset Management in connection with its takeover bid for Inter Pipeline Ltd. for approximately C$16 billion
- Antarctica Capital in its acquisition of UrtheCast’s Geosys agriculture analytics business and its UrtheDaily satellite constellation project
- Magris Performance Materials in its definitive agreement to acquire substantially all the assets of Imerys Talc America Inc., Imerys Talc Vermont Inc. and Imerys Talc Canada Inc.
- TD Asset Management in the acquisition of approximately 51% of the outstanding shares of Rabbalshede Kraft AB, a leading operator in renewable energy in the Nordic region
- Instar Asset Management in its investment in Steel Reef Infrastructure Corp. and its acquisition of a portfolio of gas plants from Crescent Point Energy Corp.
- Brookfield Asset Management and GIC , the Singapore sovereign wealth fund, in the US$8.4 billion acquisition of Genesee & Wyoming Inc., a US freight railroad owner and operator
- TD Asset Management and IST3 Investment Foundation in their C$1.7 billion agreement to acquire up to 100% of Alberta PowerLine from Canadian Utilities Limited and Quanta Services Inc.
- TD Asset Management in the acquisition of JLIF Holdings (Project Service) US, Inc. and its 100% subsidiary Project Services LLC, which operates the Connecticut Service Plazas, by Applegreen plc
- Cortland Partners an Atlanta-based multi-family real estate company, in its US$1.2 billion acquisition of Pure Multi-Family REIT LP
- Mubadala Capital and Softbank in the acquisition of Impark from Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan Board
- Instar Asset Management, JP Morgan Asset Management, Partners Group AG, Kilmer Van Nostrand Co. Limited in its acquisition of Billy Bishop Toronto City Airport and subsequent sale by Instar, Partners Group and Kilmer in the sale of their interest in Billy Bishop Toronto City Airport to JP Morgan Asset Management
- Brookfield Asset Management in its C$4.3 billion take private transaction of Enercare Inc. (including Metergy Solutions)
- TD Asset Management in its acquisition of a portfolio of nine eReserve battery energy storage facilities in Alberta, from a private equity fund managed by Kensington Capital Advisors Inc., to create Enfinite Energy, a dynamic developer, owner, and operator in the power generation and energy storage sectors
- Instar Asset Management in its acquisition of AMPORTS Inc. and its acquisition of Skyservice Investments Inc.
- Slate Canadian Real Estate Opportunity Fund I in its C$1.14 billion acquisition of a portfolio of properties from Cominar REIT

#### TECHNOLOGY, HEALTHCARE & INDUSTRIALS
- Hg Capital in its investment in Intelerad Holdings Inc. and subsequent US$2.3 billion sale to GE HealthCare
- The Management Shareholders of 9 Story Media Group in connection with Scholastic’s US$182 million investment in 9 Story Media Group
- MPM BioImpact in its acquisition of Reunion Neuroscience Inc.
- PRT Growing Services in its acquisition of International Forest Company
- Partners Group (USA) in its acquisition of SureWerx from The Riverside Company
- Power Sustainable Lios in GoodLeaf Farm’s C$150 million financing
- Boyd Gaming Corporation on its acquisition of Pala Interactive LLC for US$170 million
- Vision Ridge Partners in acquiring 100% of the equity interests in Vegpro International
- Instar Asset Management in its acquisition of Greenwood Mushrooms Development Corp.
- Trinity Capital Partners in its acquisition of the first tranche of PowerBand Solutions Inc.
- CVC Capital Partners in its definitive agreement to acquire a majority interest in ExamWorks
- Instar Asset Management in its acquisition of PRT Growing Services from private equity firm TriWest Capital Partners
- Harbin Pharmaceutical Group , an affiliate of CITIC Capital (China), in its US$760 million bid to acquire GNC Holdings Inc.
- Acuity Brands , a leading industrial technology company and the largest North American manufacturer of lighting solutions, in its acquisition of The Luminaires Group
- Permira Funds in the acquisition of the Service Provider Video Software Solutions group from Cisco, and the subsequent creation of Synamedia
